original_abstract: We present a comprehensive analysis of protein adsorption onto nanocapsules, investigating the formation of both "hard" and "soft" protein coronas. By employing a dual-methodological approach, we characterize these interactions using techniques that analyze the nanocapsules directly within the complex protein medium as well as those requiring extraction. This comparative framework allows for a more nuanced understanding of how isolation procedures affect corona composition and provides deeper insights into the interfacial behavior of nanocapsules in biological environments.
humanized_abstract: We investigated here the adsorption of different proteins onto nanocapsules in order to get insight into the constitution of both hard and soft protein corona. To this end, we used a dual-methodological approach characterized by the use of either direct or extraction methods to study protein adsorption onto nanocapsules (i) directly in solution, (ii) after extraction from the solution. The rationale behind this methodology is to understand the effect of the extraction step on the final protein corona composition and to get detailed information on the nanosurface behavior at the interface with biological media.
